value.body:
Found during a follow-up static issue scan of the Cycle desktop renderer.

Problem:
The chat panel tracks pending command acknowledgements, but when the WebSocket closes or the component unmounts it simply clears the pending command map. Callers waiting for acknowledgement callbacks never receive a failure signal, so UI actions can appear to hang or silently disappear across reconnects.

Evidence:
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/ChatPanel.tsx:520` stores pending command acknowledgements in `pendingCommandsRef`.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/ChatPanel.tsx:539-545` registers pending acknowledgements when commands are sent.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/ChatPanel.tsx:616-618` resolves/removes a pending command only when an acknowledgement message arrives.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/ChatPanel.tsx:927-933` handles socket close and immediately clears `pendingCommandsRef` before scheduling reconnect.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/ChatPanel.tsx:939-945` also clears pending commands on cleanup.

Suggested remediation:
- Model pending commands with success and failure callbacks or promises, not success-only callbacks.
- On socket close/unmount, reject or notify all pending commands with a disconnect/cancelled error before clearing the map.
- Consider resending idempotent commands after reconnect only where the command type is explicitly safe.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pending command callers receive a deterministic failure/cancel signal when the socket closes before ack.
- UI actions using `sendCommand(..., onAck)` can show an error or roll back optimistic state.
- Tests cover ack success and disconnect-before-ack behavior.

frontmatter.title: Reject pending chat commands when the WebSocket disconnects

value.title: Reject pending chat commands when the WebSocket disconnects
